originalTitle: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
title: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
content: Biniam Solomon was known for his witty and critical cartoons before the free press was closed.

originalTitle: Death toll from Ethiopia floods and landslides rises to more than 100
title: Big Rains and Helping Hands in Ethiopia
content:
Hello, my amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you important news from our wonderful continent of Africa. Today, we are going to talk about something that happened in a country called Ethiopia. Ethiopia is a beautiful country, just like where you live, filled with kind people and amazing places. Sometimes, very strong rain can fall from the sky. In Ethiopia, there was a lot, lot of rain. So much rain came down that it caused something called floods. Imagine a big puddle, but much, much bigger, covering houses and roads! This happened recently, and it made many people sad.

What Happened in Ethiopia?

In Ethiopia, too much rain fell for many days. The rain kept coming and coming. When too much water comes, it can be very dangerous. It covered many places where people live and play. This big rain caused two big problems: floods and landslides. A flood is when water covers the land where it should not be. A landslide is when big piles of dirt and rocks slide down a hill very fast. Sadly, because of these floods and landslides, many people lost their lives. More than 100 people died. This makes our hearts feel sad for them and their families. We send big hugs to everyone in Ethiopia who is hurting.

What Are Floods?

Imagine your bathtub filling up with water. Now imagine if the water kept coming out and went all over your bathroom floor! That's a little bit like a flood, but much bigger and outside. Floods happen when there is too much rain. The rivers get too full, and the water spills out. It covers roads, houses, and farms. It can make it hard for people to go to school or get food. **Did you know?** Rain is very important for our world! It helps plants grow, fills our rivers, and gives us water to drink. But too much rain all at once can be a problem.

What Are Landslides?

Have you ever made a sandcastle at the beach and then watched it crumble down? A landslide is a bit like that, but with big hills and mountains. When there is too much rain, the ground can get very, very wet. It becomes soft and heavy. If it's on a hill, the wet dirt and rocks can start to slide down very fast. This is a landslide. Landslides can push over trees and houses. They are very powerful and can happen suddenly, which makes them very dangerous for people who live near hills. **Did you know?** Trees can help stop landslides! Their roots hold the soil tightly, like glue, stopping it from sliding down easily. That's why planting trees is so important!

Why Did This Happen? (Stronger Storms)

The people who study the weather, like super smart detectives for the sky, say that storms are getting bigger and stronger. They call these "intense" storms. This means when it rains, it doesn't just sprinkle a little. It pours and pours very hard. And when the wind blows, it blows very, very strongly. Imagine a little garden hose, then imagine a giant fire hose! That's how much more water is coming down in some of these new, stronger storms.

What is Our Earth Getting Warmer? (Global Warming)

Some of these big, strong storms are happening because our Earth is getting a little bit warmer. This is something called "global warming" or "climate change." Imagine our Earth wearing a blanket that is getting thicker and thicker. This blanket traps more heat from the sun. When the Earth gets warmer, it can change the weather. It can make some places hotter and drier, and other places get more rain than before. This extra warmth in the air can make storms much bigger and stronger. **Did you know?** Our Earth is like our big home! We need to take good care of it, just like we take care of our own houses.

Helping Hands All Around

When something sad like this happens, people all over the world want to help. People in Ethiopia are helping each other, like good neighbours and friends. They are helping to find people, give them food, and find safe places to stay. People from other countries are also sending help. It shows that even when bad things happen, people are kind and care for one another. We should always remember to be kind and help others, especially when they need it most. Every little bit of help counts!

What Can We Do?

Even though we are kids, we can still do our part! We can learn about why our Earth is getting warmer. We can try to save energy at home. Maybe turn off lights when you leave a room. Or tell your parents to switch off the TV when no one is watching it. These small things help our Earth stay healthy. We can also draw pictures or write letters of hope for the children in Ethiopia. Knowing that others are thinking of them can bring a smile. **Did you know?** Africa is a huge continent with many different countries, languages, and cultures! Ethiopia is one of these amazing countries.

A Message of Hope

Even though this news is sad, we must remember that people are strong. People in Ethiopia are working hard to make things better again. We send our love and strength to all the children and families in Ethiopia. Let's all think about how we can take care of our Earth and help each other. Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends. See you next time with more news from our amazing continent!
summary: Heavy rains caused floods and landslides in Ethiopia, leading to many people losing their lives, but people are helping each other and learning about why storms are getting stronger.
whyMatters: This is important because we need to understand how big rains can hurt people and how a warmer Earth can make storms stronger, so we can all help take care of our world and each other.

title: Fastest to 100 wins - Europa specialist Emery sets Villa record

originalTitle: 'Not appropriate' for Iran to be at World Cup - Trump
title: 'Not appropriate' for Iran to be at World Cup - Trump
content: US president Donald Trump says Iran are "welcome" at the 2026 World Cup but it is not "appropriate" for them to be there.
